category_title: Struggling readers who view reading as a process of remembering and repeating
minimal_context: Alyssa is asked to read back a shared writing journal entry she copied from the board during a whole-class literacy lesson.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Alyssa, please bring your shared writing journal here. Let's read the entry you just copied from the board.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Sits quietly and watches the teacher's face, rarely focusing on the print in the journal.) First my mom reads it to me, and then I can read it.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think it says? Give it a try.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: To do it over and over again. 'Cause if you do it over and over again, you'll know how to read.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, look at the words you copied and read them to me.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Alyssa, please bring your shared writing journal here. Let's read the entry you just copied from the board.

STUDENT: (Sits quietly and watches the teacher's face, rarely focusing on the print in the journal.) First my mom reads it to me, and then I can read it.

TUTOR: What do you think it says? Give it a try.

STUDENT: To do it over and over again. 'Cause if you do it over and over again, you'll know how to read.

TUTOR: Okay, look at the words you copied and read them to me.

STUDENT: The /b/-/a/-/b/-yuh came…

task.content: Observing a whole-class phonics lesson where students are changing letters to make different words in the /an/ family on a magnet board, and reading back a shared writing journal entry she had copied from the board
task.learning_goal: changing letters to make different words in the /an/ family

context.materials: magnet board, shared writing journal, library books

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When asked to read back a shared writing journal entry she had copied from the board
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: began to read: 'The /b/-/a/-/b/-yuh came….' Alyssa also read library books in this manner, occasionally inserting words she knew at sight.

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: sits quietly, silently mouthing words as they are read by others. She watches Mrs. Parks but does not look at the board... rarely focuses on the print.
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Consistent with this view of reading as remembering
related_quotes: To do it over and over again,” Alyssa explained. “’Cause if you do it over and over again, you’ll know how to read.
First my mom reads it to me, and then I can read it,
